title: VPN Concurrent Sessions
spl:
index=vpn sourcetype="cisco:asa"
| where action="session_connect" OR action="session_disconnect"
| timechart span=15m dc(user) as concurrent_users
description: VPN capacity planning prevents remote workers from being locked out. Trending identifies peak usage and growth patterns.
value: VPN capacity planning prevents remote workers from being locked out. Trending identifies peak usage and growth patterns.
implementation: Track VPN session connects/disconnects. Calculate concurrent users over time. Alert when approaching license or capacity limits. Report on peak usage patterns for capacity planning. Track growth trends.
visualization: Line chart (concurrent sessions), Gauge (% of capacity), Single value (current active sessions), Area chart (sessions over time).
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic
  by All_Traffic.user, All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest span=1h
| sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Anomalies from new SaaS app rollouts, browser updates, or vendor URL category changes.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
â€¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_cisco-asa`, `Splunk_TA_paloalto` (GlobalProtect), `TA-fortinet_fortigate`, `Splunk_TA_juniper`.
â€¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: VPN concentrator session logs.
â€¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 â€” Configure data collection
Track VPN session connects/disconnects. Calculate concurrent users over time. Alert when approaching license or capacity limits. Report on peak usage patterns for capacity planning. Track growth trends.

Step 2 â€” Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=vpn sourcetype="cisco:asa"
| where action="session_connect" OR action="session_disconnect"
| timechart span=15m dc(user) as concurrent_users
```

Understanding this SPL

**VPN Concurrent Sessions** â€” VPN capacity planning prevents remote workers from being locked out. Trending identifies peak usage and growth patterns.

Documented **Data sources**: VPN concentrator session logs. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_cisco-asa`, `Splunk_TA_paloalto` (GlobalProtect), `TA-fortinet_fortigate`, `Splunk_TA_juniper`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feedâ€”rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: vpn; **sourcetype**: cisco:asa. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

â€¢ Scopes the data: index=vpn, sourcetype="cisco:asa". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
â€¢ Filters the current rows with `where action="session_connect" OR action="session_disconnect"` â€” typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
â€¢ `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=15m** buckets â€” ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.

Step 3 â€” Validate
Compare with Palo Alto Networks Prisma Access or firewall manager for the same users, rules, and time window.


Step 4 â€” Operationalize

Step 4 â€” Operationalize
Step 4 â€” Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (concurrent sessions), Gauge (% of capacity), Single value (current active sessions), Area chart (sessions over time).
grandmaExplanation: We use network security tools to watch who accesses what, help prevent data leaks, enforce network isolation, and secure cloud and remote access.
hardware: Cisco ASA 5506-X/5508-X/5516-X/5525-X/5545-X/5555-X, ASAv, Cisco Secure Firewall 3100/4200; Palo Alto PA-220/PA-440/PA-3200/PA-5200, GlobalProtect; Fortinet FortiGate 60F/100F/200F/600F/1800F, FortiGate SSL-VPN; Juniper SRX300/SRX1500/SRX4100/SRX4200, Junos Dynamic VPN
